turns.text: Plants release oxygen during photosynthesis, as a byproduct of turning carbon dioxide and water into glucose using light.
is this a piece of cake to remember?
It can be! Just remember: plants take in carbon dioxide and give out oxygen. A simple way to recall it.
In a fraction, the denominator (bottom) is the number of equal parts the whole is split into, and the numerator (top) is how many of those parts you have.
